deadline_note: 2026 deadline is Aug 3, 6pm ET (movable window July 28-Aug 3)

candidates.name: Tarik Skubal
candidates.context: Downgrade from lock status: Tigers are 22-14 since June 1 and within 3.5 games of a playoff spot, and 'Tigers keeping Skubal' chatter is building (heavy.com, ~Jul 17; MLB.com Jul 15). Cubs/Braves/Rays strongest suitors if he moves; Dodgers a wild card seen as underselling interest.

candidates.name: Aroldis Chapman
candidates.context: Boston 11-1 in July with a 10-game win streak; Morosi says a Chapman trade 'is increasingly unlikely to happen at all' (heavy.com/MLB Network, Jul 17). 2026 AL All-Star closer; Sox now lean keep-or-buy. Was 90% on ESPN's Jul 6 board.

candidates.name: Freddy Peralta
candidates.context: Still the most obvious Mets sell piece — near-lock to move despite a 4.81 ERA; Cubs and White Sox most heavily linked (heavy.com/SI, mid-Jul). Mets formally in sell mode with only Soto, Benge, Ewing, McLean and Scott off-limits (SNY via FOX, Jul 14).

candidates.name: Luis Arraez
candidates.context: Hitting .330 (2nd in MLB) on an expiring $12M deal for a bad Giants team; says he's open to an SF extension but beat coverage treats him as the likeliest position player moved, with Yankees and Rays the top fits (Press Democrat Jul 17; MLBTR; heavy.com).

candidates.name: Ryan Jeffers
candidates.context: Was 85% on ESPN's Jul 6 board, but Minnesota's buyer behavior (Nance acquisition Jul 10 for the bullpen) softens the sell case; still the most movable Twins piece if they thread both needles.

candidates.name: Gleyber Torres
candidates.context: Reinvented leadoff man on a selling Tigers club; 80% trade odds, oblique strain slightly clouds market.

candidates.name: Taylor Ward
candidates.context: Rental corner bat with a career-high .389 OBP; widely reported as very likely to be dealt.

candidates.name: Casey Mize
candidates.context: Same Tigers logic as Skubal: 22-14 since June 1 and 3.5 out — a controllable starter is even harder to pry loose from a team back in the race (Tigers surge reporting, Jul 14-17). Was 60% on Jul 6.

candidates.name: Joe Ryan
candidates.context: Conflicting signals: Twins acted as buyers with the Nance deal (Jul 10), but Joe Ryan-to-Phillies rumors persist in the wide-open AL (heavy.com, ~Jul 17). Down from 55% coin-flip to genuinely uncertain.

candidates.name: Seth Lugo
candidates.context: Steady mid-rotation arm on selling Royals; signed through 2027 but ESPN gives 65% trade odds.

candidates.name: Robbie Ray
candidates.context: Giants beat expects Arraez and Ray to be the two who go (Press Democrat, Jul 17); newest fit is St. Louis (50-45, one back of the final NL WC), though his $25M salary narrows the market.

candidates.name: Kenley Jansen
candidates.context: Veteran closer on a selling Tigers team; disappointing 4.00 ERA but obvious rental-type piece.

candidates.name: Antonio Senzatela
candidates.context: Converted reliever with a 2.13 ERA and new cutter/velo spike; Rockies always sell and he is coveted.

candidates.name: Kyle Finnegan
candidates.context: Rental late-inning reliever on a selling Tigers club; on multiple others-to-watch lists.

candidates.name: A.J. Minter
candidates.context: Rental lefty reliever on a selling Mets team; strong demand reported for left-handed relief.

candidates.name: Brooks Raley
candidates.context: Second rental lefty the Mets are expected to move alongside Minter.

candidates.name: Nathaniel Lowe
candidates.context: Rental bat found on a minor-league deal, .256/.350/.477; easy flip for the selling Reds.

candidates.name: Eugenio Suarez
candidates.context: Rental power bat on a selling Reds club; named a rental candidate by CBS and ranked by ESPN.

candidates.name: Brady Singer
candidates.context: Impending free agent with a career-worst ERA but a 1.64 June; classic seller rental.

candidates.name: Tyler Stephenson
candidates.context: Rental catcher listed among the selling Reds' movable veterans by CBS.

candidates.name: Starling Marte
candidates.context: Righty-hitting rental outfielder on selling Royals; contenders seeking OF platoon help.

candidates.name: Lane Thomas
candidates.context: Righty rental outfielder the selling Royals are expected to shop alongside Marte.

candidates.name: Kris Bubic
candidates.context: Rental lefty starter on selling Royals; 4.11 ERA with walk spike after elbow/shoulder scares.

candidates.name: Pete Fairbanks
candidates.context: Rental closer CBS names as Miami's most obvious deadline chip even with the Marlins surging.

candidates.name: Michael Wacha
candidates.context: Royals will listen but are holding a sky-high ask and are reluctant to move him; Rosenthal on a team meeting the multi-top-prospect price: 'Seems doubtful' (MLBTR/Athlon, ~Jul 16). Partial no-trade protection.

candidates.name: Sonny Gray
candidates.context: Effectively off the market amid Boston's surge — 'Gray and Chapman don't look like trade candidates right now,' though a poor end of July could flip Boston back to selling (heavy.com, Jul 17; Passan calls plans 'fluid', boston.com Jul 17).

candidates.name: Sandy Alcantara
candidates.context: Direction flip: the Marlins have been the hottest team in baseball and sit in prime playoff position — they're listed among buyers now (MLB.com/Just Baseball, Jul 10-15), taking Alcantara off the realistic market.

candidates.name: Willson Contreras
candidates.context: FOX Sports deadline tracker (Jul 14) lists him as not available — declined to waive his no-trade protection — and Boston's turnaround removes the incentive to push. Notable 2026 All-Star snub, so the bat still has value if Boston collapses.

candidates.name: Christian Walker
candidates.context: Astros' return to contention (3 GB in the ALW) shelves the veteran-bat sell-off scenarios from early July (CBS/MLBTR, ~Jul 15).

candidates.name: Isaac Paredes
candidates.context: Still 'probably the most likely Houston piece moved if they soft-sell' (MLBTR outlook, ~Jul 15), but the Astros' climb back into the race drops him from 40% territory to a long shot.

candidates.name: Jeff Hoffman
candidates.context: All-Star closer with elite strikeout rates but a 4.84 ERA; Jays could reshuffle the $11M/yr deal.

candidates.name: Trevor Rogers
candidates.context: Impending free agent with a 2.22 June ERA, but Baltimore trending toward buying muddies his status.

candidates.name: Dustin May
candidates.context: Rental and the Cardinals' best recent starter; St. Louis is still on the contention fence.

candidates.name: Lars Nootbaar
candidates.context: Controlled through 2027 and hot since returning from heel surgery; moves only if Cards pivot to selling.

candidates.name: CJ Abrams
candidates.context: Career year (.287/.370/.533) with control through 2028; Nats would need to be overwhelmed — 15% odds.

candidates.name: Reid Detmers
candidates.context: Among FanGraphs WAR leaders with control through 2028, but owner Moreno reportedly resisting trades.

candidates.name: Jose Soriano
candidates.context: Hard-throwing, cheaply controlled arm who would net multiple top prospects if Moreno relents.

candidates.name: Jarren Duran
candidates.context: Two more years of control and a down offensive year mean Boston would be selling low; with the Sox back in Wild Card contention, beat coverage says he stays unless the bat wakes up AND Boston slides (SI/Yahoo, Jul 17).

candidates.name: Matt Chapman
candidates.context: Rosenthal throws cold water on SF moving its big contracts; Giants beat expects the lineup beyond Arraez/Ray to stay largely intact (ClutchPoints/Press Democrat, Jul 17).

candidates.name: Hunter Goodman
candidates.context: Leads catchers in homers and is controlled through 2029; Rockies would demand a massive return.

candidates.name: Jung Hoo Lee
candidates.context: One of three Giants leading ESPN insiders' candidate list with Arraez and Ray (McCovey Chronicles, mid-Jul), but Rosenthal is skeptical SF moves the big contracts — likelihood holds around a coin flip, below the rentals.

candidates.name: Luis Castillo
candidates.context: Mariners have six healthy starters for five spots; 5.22 ERA with under-the-hood improvement.

candidates.name: Clay Holmes
candidates.context: Converted starter rehabbing a broken fibula with a 2027 player option; Mets can trade him while he rehabs.

candidates.name: Mickey Moniak
candidates.context: Platoon corner bat (.273/.314/.539 over two years) though Coors inflates the line; controlled through 2027.

candidates.name: Jake McCarthy
candidates.context: Cheap defensive outfielder controlled through 2028 on a perennial seller.

candidates.name: Trevor Larnach
candidates.context: Patient lefty platoon bat (.276/.374/.419) heating up in June; Twins retooling around the edges.

candidates.name: Foster Griffin
candidates.context: Nationals are unexpectedly still in it — 3 games back of the final NL Wild Card — and are only 'open to listening' rather than engaging on their pieces (MLBTR/Bleacher Report, Jul 17-18). Down from 55%.

candidates.name: Justin Verlander
candidates.context: Veteran rental on a selling Tigers club; on ESPN's top-100 candidate board.

candidates.name: Jack Flaherty
candidates.context: Selling Tigers could deal him for the second straight deadline cycle; option structure in play.

candidates.name: Luis Robert Jr.
candidates.context: Perennial deadline name now on a selling Mets roster; talent-over-production gamble for buyers.

candidates.name: Mark Vientos
candidates.context: Young corner bat on the Mets' others-to-watch list as New York retools.

candidates.name: Taylor Rogers
candidates.context: Veteran lefty reliever the Twins can flip as they thread buyer/seller status.

candidates.name: Josh Bell
candidates.context: Veteran switch-hitting rental bat on ESPN's candidate board.

candidates.name: Willi Castro
candidates.context: Two-time All-Star utilityman on a selling Rockies club; fits every contender's bench.

candidates.name: Michael Lorenzen
candidates.context: Veteran swingman rental on the selling Rockies.

candidates.name: Tomoyuki Sugano
candidates.context: Strike-throwing veteran on the selling Rockies; back-end rotation rental for a contender.

candidates.name: Andrew Kittredge
candidates.context: Veteran setup rental; moves if the Orioles' buy-lean flips back to selling.

candidates.name: Chris Bassitt
candidates.context: Veteran innings rental on Baltimore's others-to-watch list.

candidates.name: Ryan Mountcastle
candidates.context: Blocked Orioles first baseman who surfaces in talks every deadline.

candidates.name: Kirby Yates
candidates.context: Veteran rental reliever on a rebuilding Angels club.

candidates.name: Jo Adell
candidates.context: Talented but streaky power bat; Angels rebuild logic says trade him, Moreno says otherwise — 20% odds.

candidates.name: Jorge Soler
candidates.context: Veteran power rental on the Angels' movable-contract list.

candidates.name: Matt Shaw
candidates.context: Five years of control but squeezed off third base in Chicago; prime trade chip for a Cubs upgrade — 40% odds.

candidates.name: JoJo Romero
candidates.context: Rental lefty reliever the Cardinals will move if they sell (or even if they soft-buy).

candidates.name: Riley O'Brien
candidates.context: Breakout Cardinals closer drawing calls; ESPN ranks him a top-30 candidate.

candidates.name: JJ Bleday
candidates.context: Breakout offensive season on a selling Reds club; controlled beyond 2026 so the price is higher.

candidates.name: Byron Buxton
candidates.context: Voted 2026 All-Star starter but sat out with the hip issue that's nagged him all season; Twins bought a reliever (Nance, Jul 10) for MLB's worst bullpen — trade scenario basically dead (Yardbarker/MLBTR, Jul 10-14).

candidates.name: Jeremy Pena
candidates.context: Astros have gotten healthy and climbed to 3 games out in the AL West (1.5 in the WC); despite moving McCullers, a Pena deal now reads as blow-it-up fantasy, not a live rumor (CBS/MLBTR outlook, ~Jul 15).

candidates.name: Steven Kwan
candidates.context: Guardians always listen on their elite contact outfielder; controlled through 2027, huge ask.

candidates.name: Adley Rutschman
candidates.context: Orioles fielded winter calls; a deadline sell-high on the former franchise catcher would stun but is on watch lists.

candidates.name: Rafael Devers
candidates.context: Giants reportedly open to moving big long-term money, but the contract makes a deal unlikely.

candidates.name: Willy Adames
candidates.context: Selling Giants would love relief from the mega-deal; contract makes him hard to move.

candidates.name: Mike Trout
candidates.context: CBS floats him as the ultimate Angels domino, but his full no-trade clause and contract make it a fantasy.

candidates.name: Kevin Gausman
candidates.context: MLB.com reports he could be made available only if the Blue Jays slide in July.

candidates.name: Seiya Suzuki
candidates.context: Cubs are firmly buyers — pursuing Skubal and Peralta-class pitching (heavy.com, ~Jul 17) — so the early-July Suzuki speculation is dead weight; drop from 15% to negligible.

candidates.name: Camilo Doval
candidates.context: On ESPN's watch list as a Yankees bullpen-surplus flip candidate despite New York buying.

candidates.name: Josh Hader
candidates.context: Signed through 2028 at $19M/yr, working back from injury, and Houston is contending again — MLBTR calls a deal unlikely even though the return would be significant (Jul 15).

candidates.name: Trevor Story
candidates.context: On ESPN's board as a contract Boston would happily offload; opt-out structure and salary limit the market.

candidates.name: Spencer Torkelson
candidates.context: Former No. 1 overall pick on Tigers others-to-watch lists as Detroit shops veterans.

candidates.name: Will Vest
candidates.context: Late-inning arm on ESPN/MLBTR Tigers watch lists as Detroit pivots to selling.

candidates.name: Zach McKinstry
candidates.context: 2025 All-Star super-utilityman; ESPN top-100 candidate on a selling club.

candidates.name: Jake Rogers

candidates.context: Perennially discussed; 2026 beat reporting says he won't be traded given bounce-back production + contract through 2030.

candidates.name: Nathan Eovaldi
candidates.context: SI five-Rangers-to-trade-if-Texas-falters list ($24M in 2027, sterling postseason record); first-place Rangers positioned as buyers.

candidates.name: Jacob deGrom
candidates.context: Could command 3-5 prospects with MLB-best K/BB and $37M owed 2027 — only if the AL West leaders collapse.

candidates.name: Joc Pederson
candidates.context: SI + Nolan Writin' sell candidate ($21.25M expiring) whose improved form makes him moveable if Texas fades.

candidates.name: Corey Seager
candidates.context: SI's 'nuclear option' — $31.5M/yr through 2030; Red Sox expressed offseason interest but their record makes it unlikely now.

candidates.name: Davis Schneider
candidates.context: Blue Jays Nation deadline preview: surplus piece with poor results and a blocked path as buying Toronto shops for a RH OF bat.

candidates.name: Cedric Mullins
candidates.context: MLBTR Rays outlook: 2026 rentals like Mullins probably dangled even as Tampa buys elsewhere in its usual churn.

candidates.name: Steven Matz
candidates.context: Struggling veteran on an expiring deal, listed among rentals likely dangled before Aug 3.

candidates.name: Garrett Cleavinger
candidates.context: MLBTR: $2.4M lefty with one arb year left could be swapped for OF help if Rays trust Booser in the role.

candidates.name: Gavin Lux
candidates.context: Among dangle-able Rays rentals, but ankle/shoulder injuries wiped his season; rehab targeting the All-Star break.

candidates.name: Jake Fraley
candidates.context: Dangle-able expiring-control OF, though on the IL post-hernia procedure, limiting value.

candidates.name: Nick Martinez
candidates.context: On the Rays' expiring-control dangle list, though MLBTR deems a midseason deal unlikely to return much.

candidates.name: Jake Burger
candidates.context: Change-of-scenery coverage: 63 HR in 2023-24 but just 10 homers and a .705 OPS this season in Texas.

candidates.name: Munetaka Murakami
candidates.context: B/R: White Sox slugger would require a 'gargantuan offer' to be moved at the deadline.

candidates.name: Luis Severino
candidates.context: ESPN's Skubal blockbuster proposals have the A's shipping Severino as the MLB piece, with his player option flagged as a consideration.

candidates.name: Lawrence Butler
candidates.context: ESPN labels him a change-of-scenery candidate and includes him in a hypothetical A's package for Skubal.

candidates.name: Spencer Jones
candidates.context: ESPN's proposed Yankees package for Skubal is headlined by the slugging Jones (plus Lagrange or Elmer Rodriguez), though a Yankees source called their chances 'none.'

candidates.name: Kevin Alcantara
candidates.context: ESPN's Cubs-for-Skubal construct includes Alcantara, who has 15 homers in 41 Triple-A games as blocked outfield surplus.

candidates.name: Jaxon Wiggins
candidates.context: SI and ESPN both name the Cubs' No. 4 prospect as a moveable high-upside arm, sidelined since April with elbow inflammation.

candidates.name: Zyhir Hope
candidates.context: ESPN's Dodgers-for-Skubal proposal centers the top-50 prospect, a 21-year-old on pace for 30 homers.

candidates.name: Emmet Sheehan
candidates.context: ESPN's proposed Dodgers package uses Sheehan as the MLB-ready starter, part of LA's 'wholly unique' pitching-and-outfield surplus.

candidates.name: Ethan Salas
candidates.context: SI lists the 20-year-old elite defensive catcher among top prospects who could be dealt, and ESPN includes him in a Padres Skubal package.

candidates.name: Gage Wood
candidates.context: SI: Phillies' 2025 first-rounder with an upper-90s fastball called a likely candidate for deadline trade discussions.

candidates.name: Felnin Celesten
candidates.context: SI: 20-year-old switch-hitting shortstop potentially expendable for Seattle with Colt Emerson entrenched at the position.

candidates.name: Mason Miller
candidates.context: Blockbuster newly in play: Yankees 'love the idea' of adding MLB's most dominant bullpen arm and Phillies chatter followed (MLB.com Jul 15; heavy.com Jul 17-18). Controllable through 2029, so open-minded San Diego's ask is a haul.

candidates.name: Ketel Marte
candidates.context: Rays linked to a Marte blockbuster on Jul 14 (FOX tracker) as Arizona weighs direction; led all non-All-Stars in total bases. Contract and D-backs' fence-sitting make it a long shot.

candidates.name: Shane Bieber
candidates.context: One of four Toronto veterans on expiring deals named as prime candidates if the Jays sell (MLB.com Blue Jays deadline plans, mid-Jul). 2020 AL Cy Young pedigree.

candidates.name: Daulton Varsho
candidates.context: Named with Gausman as the most attractive Toronto pieces if they head toward selling mode (heavy.com/Yardbarker, Jul 15-18); expiring deal, elite defense.

candidates.name: George Springer
candidates.context: On the Jays' expiring-deal sell list (MLB.com, mid-Jul), but age and salary mean Toronto may have to pay down money to move him (Lindy's).

candidates.name: Francisco Lindor
candidates.context: Newly, genuinely available — only Soto and four young players are off-limits (SNY's Janes, ~Jul 15) — but $160M over five years, a limited NTC, a .210/.291/.363 line and his desire to stay make insiders call a deal unlikely (amNY Jul 17; BR Jul 18).

candidates.name: Spencer Steer
candidates.context: Drawing interest from multiple teams with the last-place Reds selling (FOX tracker, Jul 14); positional flexibility widens the market.
